title: Build Specification (canonical Intermediate Representation)
description: The ONE artifact the SQLX Generator is allowed to read. Produced by the Mapping Resolver. Every field the SQLX Generator needs to render read/process/write.sqlx must be present here — the generator must never re-read source_schema.json, target_schema.json, sttm.json, or udf.md to fill a gap. One file per target table: metadata/build/<TABLE>.buildspec.json.

load_strategy.description: Reserved for future use per ADR-001 §6/§11. Only 'full_load' has a corresponding SQLX template today; 'incremental' and 'scd2' are accepted values with no renderer yet and MUST cause the SQLX Generator to fail loudly rather than fall back to full_load silently.

staging_table.description: Name of the intermediate_db table that read.sqlx lands into and process.sqlx transforms in place.

on.description: Fully-formed SQL boolean join predicate, already resolved to concrete column names. The SQLX Generator inserts this verbatim; it performs no further reasoning about join logic.

items.description: Fully-formed SQL boolean predicate, inserted verbatim into the WHERE clause of process.sqlx.

grain.description: One-sentence statement of the table's row grain, e.g. 'one row per <entity>'. Carried into generated SQLX as a header comment for human readers; not used by any downstream logic.

mapping_refs.description: IDs into metadata/mapping/sttm.json this buildspec was resolved from, for audit traceability.

source.description: null when transformation is CONSTANT or GENERATED (both produce a value with no source column).

expression.description: Fully-formed SQL scalar expression, required when transformation is EXPRESSION, CONSTANT, or LOOKUP; null otherwise. GENERATED columns use 'generator' instead of a raw expression string.

udf.description: UDF name from udf.md, required when transformation is UDF; null otherwise.

generator.description: Semantic description of how a GENERATED column's value is produced; null unless transformation is GENERATED. The SQLX Generator derives the actual SQL expression from this (scripts/render_sqlx.py render_generator_expression()), rather than trusting a pre-written expression string, so a generated-column's *meaning* (e.g. 'a row-number surrogate key ordered by X') stays legible in the buildspec instead of being buried in opaque SQL.

type.description: ROW_NUMBER: a surrogate key numbered by 'order_by'. SEQUENCE: pulls from a named PostgreSQL sequence. UUID: gen_random_uuid() (PostgreSQL 13+, no extension required). DEFAULT: a fixed literal, inserted verbatim like CONSTANT but semantically 'this column has no meaningful source, not even a business-constant value'.

order_by.description: Required when type is ROW_NUMBER. A bare column name on the buildspec's first-listed source_tables entry; the SQLX Generator fully qualifies it.

sequence_name.description: Required when type is SEQUENCE. Must already exist in the target database (sequence creation is a bootstrap/DDL concern, not something a buildspec provisions).

default_value.description: Required when type is DEFAULT. Inserted verbatim, exactly like a CONSTANT transformation's 'expression'.
